    97	  basePendingPath: string;
    98	  uniqueSuffix: string | null;
    99	}
   100	
   101	function parsePendingPath(filePath: string): PendingPathInfo | null {
   102	  const parseBasePendingPath = (candidatePath: string): PendingPathInfo | null => {
   103	    const dir = path.dirname(candidatePath);
   104	    const ext = path.extname(candidatePath);
   105	    const base = path.basename(candidatePath, ext);
   106	    if (!base.endsWith(PENDING_SUFFIX)) {
   107	      return null;
   108	    }
   109	
   110	    const originalBase = base.slice(0, -PENDING_SUFFIX.length);
   111	    return {
   112	      originalPath: path.join(dir, `${originalBase}${ext}`),
   113	      basePendingPath: candidatePath,
   114	      uniqueSuffix: null,
   115	    };
   116	  };
   117	
   118	  const suffixExt = path.extname(filePath);
   119	  if (suffixExt) {
   120	    const uniqueSuffix = suffixExt.slice(1);
   121	    const basePendingPath = filePath.slice(0, -suffixExt.length);
   122	    const suffixedMatch = parseBasePendingPath(basePendingPath);
   123	    if (
   124	      suffixedMatch &&
   125	      path.extname(suffixedMatch.basePendingPath) &&
   126	      PENDING_UNIQUE_SUFFIX_PATTERN.test(uniqueSuffix)
   127	    ) {
   128	      return {
   129	        ...suffixedMatch,
   130	        uniqueSuffix,
   131	      };
   132	    }
   133	  }
   134	
   135	  return parseBasePendingPath(filePath);
   136	}
   137	
   138	function isPendingFile(filePath: string): boolean {
   139	  return parsePendingPath(filePath) !== null;
   140	}
   141	
   142	function getOriginalPath(pendingPath: string): string {
   143	  return parsePendingPath(pendingPath)?.originalPath ?? pendingPath;
   144	}
   145	
   146	function runInTransaction<T>(database: Database.Database, callback: () => T): T {
   147	  const runCallback = (): T => {
   148	    const depth = activeTransactions.get(database) ?? 0;
   149	    activeTransactions.set(database, depth + 1);
   150	    try {
   151	      return callback();
   152	    } finally {
   153	      const nextDepth = (activeTransactions.get(database) ?? 1) - 1;
   154	      if (nextDepth > 0) {
   155	        activeTransactions.set(database, nextDepth);
   156	      } else {
   157	        activeTransactions.delete(database);
   158	      }
   159	    }
   160	  };
   161	
   162	  if ((activeTransactions.get(database) ?? 0) > 0 || database.inTransaction) {
   163	    return runCallback();
   164	  }
   165	
   166	  return database.transaction(() => runCallback())();
   167	}
   168	
   169	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   170	   6. ATOMIC FILE OPERATIONS
   171	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   172	
   173	/**
   174	 * Write a file atomically using write-to-temp-then-rename pattern.
   175	 * This prevents partial writes if the process crashes mid-write.
   176	 */
   177	function atomicWriteFile(filePath: string, content: string, encoding: BufferEncoding = 'utf-8'): boolean {
   178	  const tempPath = filePath + TEMP_SUFFIX;
   179	
   180	  try {
   181	    // Ensure directory exists
   182	    const dir = path.dirname(filePath);
   183	    if (!fs.existsSync(dir)) {
   184	      fs.mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true });
   185	    }
   186	
   187	    // Write to temp file
   188	    fs.writeFileSync(tempPath, content, encoding);
   189	
   190	    // Atomic rename
   191	    fs.renameSync(tempPath, filePath);
   192	
   193	    metrics.totalAtomicWrites++;
   194	    metrics.lastOperationTime = new Date().toISOString();
   195	    return true;
   196	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   197	    const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   198	    console.warn(`[transaction-manager] atomicWriteFile error: ${msg}`);
   199	    metrics.totalErrors++;
   200	
   201	    // Clean up temp file
   202	    try {
   203	      if (fs.existsSync(tempPath)) fs.unlinkSync(tempPath);
   204	    } catch { /* ignore cleanup errors */ }
   205	
   206	    return false;
   207	  }
   208	}
   209	
   210	/**
   211	 * Delete a file if it exists.
   212	 */
   213	function deleteFileIfExists(filePath: string): boolean {
   214	  try {
   215	    if (fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
   216	      fs.unlinkSync(filePath);
   217	      metrics.totalDeletes++;
   218	      metrics.lastOperationTime = new Date().toISOString();
   219	      return true;
   220	    }
   221	    return false;
   222	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   223	    const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   224	    console.warn(`[transaction-manager] deleteFileIfExists error: ${msg}`);
   225	    metrics.totalErrors++;
   226	    return false;
   227	  }
   228	}
   229	
   230	/**
   231	 * Execute an atomic save with pending file pattern.
   232	 * 1. Write to pending path
   233	 * 2. Execute database operations
   234	 * 3. Rename pending to final
   235	 *
   236	 * This function provides file-system-level atomicity (write-to-pending + rename),
   237	 * NOT database transaction isolation. Callers needing transactional guarantees for
   238	 * dbOperation() must wrap it in their own db.transaction() call. The rename step
   239	 * ensures the file is either fully written or not present, but the DB operation
   240	 * is not rolled back if the rename fails (extremely rare on local filesystems).
   241	 */
   242	function executeAtomicSave(
   243	  filePath: string,
   244	  content: string,
   245	  dbOperation: () => void
   246	): AtomicSaveResult {
   247	  const pendingPath = getPendingPath(filePath);
   248	
   249	  try {
   250	    // Step 1: Write to pending path
   251	    const dir = path.dirname(pendingPath);
   252	    if (!fs.existsSync(dir)) {
   253	      fs.mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true });
   254	    }
   255	    fs.writeFileSync(pendingPath, content, 'utf-8');
   256	
   257	    // Flag-based rollback coordination.
   258	    // The dbOperation callback runs synchronously (better-sqlite3 transaction). If it
   259	    // Succeeds but the rename (Step 3) fails, the DB change is already committed and
   260	    // Cannot be rolled back. The dbCommitted flag tracks this state so the error path
   260	    // Cannot be rolled back. The dbCommitted flag tracks this state so the error path
   261	    // Can report which step failed. No SAVEPOINT is used — better-sqlite3 transactions
   262	    // Are all-or-nothing within the dbOperation() callback itself.
   263	    let dbCommitted = false;
   264	
   265	    // Step 2: Execute database operation
   266	    try {
   267	      dbOperation();
   268	      dbCommitted = true;
   269	    } catch (dbError: unknown) {
   270	      // Database failed - clean up pending file
   271	      try { fs.unlinkSync(pendingPath); } catch { /* ignore */ }
   272	      const msg = dbError instanceof Error ? dbError.message : String(dbError);
   273	      metrics.totalErrors++;
   274	      return { success: false, filePath, error: `DB operation failed: ${msg}` };
   275	    }
   276	
   277	    // Step 3: Rename pending to final (atomic)
   278	    // P1-020 KNOWN LIMITATION: If renameSync fails after dbOperation() committed,
   279	    // The DB contains the new state but the file is not at its final path. This is
   280	    // A window of vulnerability that cannot be eliminated without two-phase commit.
   281	    // The `dbCommitted` flag on the returned error result enables callers to detect
   282	    // This state and trigger recovery (e.g., re-index from DB or replay the write).
   283	    // Mitigation: `recoverAllPendingFiles()` can be called on startup to find
   284	    // Orphaned pending files and rename them to their final paths.
   285	    try {
   286	      fs.renameSync(pendingPath, filePath);
   287	    } catch (renameError: unknown) {
   288	      // Rename failed after DB committed — DB has new state but file wasn't renamed.
   289	      // Leave pending file for startup recovery and report failure with dbCommitted flag.
   290	      const msg = renameError instanceof Error ? renameError.message : String(renameError);
   291	      console.warn(`[transaction-manager] rename failed after DB commit; pending file kept for recovery: ${pendingPath} (${msg})`);
   292	      metrics.totalErrors++;
   293	      return { success: false, filePath, error: `Rename failed after DB commit: ${msg}`, dbCommitted };
   294	    }
   295	
   296	    metrics.totalAtomicWrites++;
   297	    metrics.lastOperationTime = new Date().toISOString();
   298	    return { success: true, filePath };
   299	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   300	    const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   301	    metrics.totalErrors++;
   302	
   303	    // Clean up pending file
   304	    try {
   305	      if (fs.existsSync(pendingPath)) fs.unlinkSync(pendingPath);
   306	    } catch { /* ignore */ }
   307	
   308	    return { success: false, filePath, error: msg };
   309	  }
   310	}
   311	
   312	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   313	   7. RECOVERY
   314	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   315	
   316	/**
   317	 * Find pending files in a directory (crash recovery).
   318	 */
   319	function listFilesRecursive(dirPath: string): string[] {
   320	  const files: string[] = [];
   321	  const stack: string[] = [dirPath];
   322	
   323	  while (stack.length > 0) {
   324	    const current = stack.pop();
   325	    if (!current) continue;
   326	
   327	    const entries = fs.readdirSync(current, { withFileTypes: true, encoding: 'utf-8' });
   328	    for (const entry of entries) {
   329	      const fullPath = path.join(current, entry.name);
   330	      if (entry.isDirectory()) {
   331	        stack.push(fullPath);
   332	      } else if (entry.isFile()) {
   333	        files.push(fullPath);
   334	      }
   335	    }
   336	  }
   337	
   338	  return files;
   339	}
   340	
   341	function findPendingFiles(dirPath: string): string[] {
   342	  try {
   343	    if (!fs.existsSync(dirPath)) return [];
   344	
   345	    let files: string[];
   346	    try {
   347	      files = (fs.readdirSync(dirPath, { recursive: true, encoding: 'utf-8' }) as string[]).map((f) => path.join(dirPath, f));
   348	    } catch {
   349	      // Node 18 compatibility fallback when recursive readdir is unavailable.
   350	      files = listFilesRecursive(dirPath);
   351	    }
   352	
   353	    return files.filter((filePath) => parsePendingPath(filePath) !== null && fs.statSync(filePath).isFile());
   354	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   355	    const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   356	    console.warn(`[transaction-manager] findPendingFiles error: ${msg}`);
   357	    return [];
   358	  }
   359	}
   360	
   361	/**
   362	 * Recover a single pending file by renaming to its original path.
   363	 */
   364	function recoverPendingFile(
   365	  pendingPath: string,
   366	  isCommittedInDb?: IsCommittedCheck,
   367	  databasePathOverride?: string
   368	): RecoveryResult {
   369	  try {
   370	    // Guard: verify pending file still exists before attempting recovery
   371	    if (!fs.existsSync(pendingPath)) {
   372	      return { path: pendingPath, recovered: false, error: 'Pending file no longer exists' };
   373	    }
   374	    const pendingInfo = parsePendingPath(pendingPath);
   375	    if (!pendingInfo) {
   376	      return { path: pendingPath, recovered: false, error: 'File does not match pending-file naming contract' };
   377	    }
   378	
   379	    const originalPath = pendingInfo.originalPath;
   380	    const originalExists = fs.existsSync(originalPath);
   381	    let committedInDb: boolean | null = null;
   382	    if (isCommittedInDb) {
   383	      const databasePath = databasePathOverride ?? resolveDatabasePaths().databasePath;
   384	      if (!fs.existsSync(databasePath)) {
   385	        console.warn(`[transaction-manager] Skipping pending recovery because DB file is missing: ${databasePath}`);
   386	        return { path: pendingPath, recovered: false, error: 'Database file missing during recovery' };
   387	      }
   388	      committedInDb = isCommittedInDb(originalPath);
   389	    }
   390	
   391	    // If original exists and is newer, delete pending
   392	    if (originalExists) {
   393	      const pendingStats = fs.statSync(pendingPath);
   394	      const originalStats = fs.statSync(originalPath);
   395	      if (originalStats.mtimeMs > pendingStats.mtimeMs) {
   396	        fs.unlinkSync(pendingPath);
   397	        return { path: pendingPath, recovered: false, error: 'Original is newer' };
   398	      }
   399	    }
   400	
   401	    // Stale detection: if DB check is provided and row was never committed,
   402	    // Log and leave for manual review instead of renaming.
   403	    if (committedInDb === false) {
   404	      console.warn(`[transaction-manager] Stale pending file detected (no committed DB row): ${pendingPath}`);
   405	      return { path: pendingPath, recovered: false, error: 'Stale pending file: DB row not committed' };
   406	    }
   407	
   408	    // Rename pending to original
   409	    fs.renameSync(pendingPath, originalPath);
   410	    metrics.totalRecoveries++;
   411	    metrics.lastOperationTime = new Date().toISOString();
   412	
   413	    return { path: pendingPath, recovered: true };
   414	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   415	    const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   416	    metrics.totalErrors++;
   417	    return { path: pendingPath, recovered: false, error: msg };
   418	  }
   419	}
   420	
   421	/**
   422	 * Recover all pending files in a directory.
   423	 */
   424	function recoverAllPendingFiles(
   425	  dirPath: string,
   426	  isCommittedInDb?: IsCommittedCheck,
   427	  databasePathOverride?: string
   428	): RecoveryResult[] {
   429	  const pendingFiles = findPendingFiles(dirPath);
   430	  return pendingFiles.map((f) => recoverPendingFile(f, isCommittedInDb, databasePathOverride));
   431	}
   432	
   433	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   434	   8. EXPORTS
   435	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   436	
   437	export {
   438	  PENDING_SUFFIX,
   439	  TEMP_SUFFIX,
   440	
   441	  // Metrics
   442	  getMetrics,
   443	  resetMetrics,
   444	
   445	  // Path helpers
   446	  getPendingPath,
   447	  isPendingFile,
   448	  getOriginalPath,
   449	  runInTransaction,
   450	
   451	  // Atomic operations
   452	  atomicWriteFile,
   453	  deleteFileIfExists,
   454	  executeAtomicSave,
   455	
   456	  // Recovery
   457	  findPendingFiles,
   458	  recoverPendingFile,
   459	  recoverAllPendingFiles,
   460	};
   461	
   462	/**
   463	 * Re-exports related public types.
   464	 */
   465	export type {
   466	  TransactionMetrics,
   467	  AtomicSaveResult,
   468	  RecoveryResult,
   469	  IsCommittedCheck,
   470	};

   890	
   891	##### `shared_space_upsert`
   892	
   893	Create or update a shared-memory space. Shared spaces start locked: nobody can read or write until you add members with `shared_space_membership_set`. The person or agent who creates the space automatically becomes its owner.
   894	
   895	| Parameter | Type | Notes |
   896	|-----------|------|-------|
   897	| `spaceId` | string | **Required.** Unique identifier for the space |
   898	| `tenantId` | string | **Required.** Tenant scope |
   899	| `name` | string | **Required.** Human-readable name |
   900	| `actorUserId` | string | Caller identity (user). Provide exactly one actor |
   901	| `actorAgentId` | string | Caller identity (agent). Provide exactly one actor |
   902	| `rolloutEnabled` | boolean | Enable or disable this space |
   903	| `rolloutCohort` | string | Limit access to a specific cohort |
   904	| `killSwitch` | boolean | Emergency shutoff |
   905	
   906	---
   907	
   908	##### `shared_space_membership_set`
   909	
   910	Control who can access a shared space. Assign owner, editor or viewer roles. Only existing owners can change membership.

  1510	
  1511	/* --- 10. ATOMIC MEMORY SAVE --- */
  1512	
  1513	/**
  1514	 * Save memory content to disk with retry + rollback guarded indexing.
  1515	 *
  1516	 * The file write promotes a pending file while holding the per-spec-folder
  1517	 * mutex so concurrent saves cannot overwrite each other between disk
  1518	 * promotion and indexing. If indexing later fails, the original file content
  1519	 * is restored before the error is returned and before the lock is released.
  1520	 */
  1521	async function atomicSaveMemory(params: AtomicSaveParams, options: AtomicSaveOptions = {}): Promise<AtomicSaveResult> {
  1522	  const { file_path, content } = params;
  1523	  const { force = false } = options;
  1524	  const database = requireDb();
  1525	  // Use unique suffix to prevent concurrent pending-file race (F01-003)
  1526	  const basePendingPath = transactionManager.getPendingPath(file_path);
  1527	  const pendingPath = `${basePendingPath}.${randomUUID().slice(0, 8)}`;
  1528	  const originalState = captureAtomicSaveOriginalState(file_path);
  1529	
  1530	  let indexResult: IndexResult | null = null;
  1531	  let indexError: Error | null = null;
  1532	  let validationError: ReturnType<typeof memoryParser.validateParsedMemory> | null = null;
  1533	  let restoredOriginalAfterFailure = false;
  1534	  let errorMetadata: Record<string, string> | null = null;
  1535	  const mergeErrorMetadata = (entry: Record<string, string> | null): void => {
  1536	    if (!entry) {
  1537	      return;
  1538	    }
  1539	    errorMetadata = {
  1540	      ...(errorMetadata ?? {}),
  1541	      ...entry,
  1542	    };
  1543	  };
  1544	  const maxIndexAttempts = 2;
  1545	  for (let attempt = 1; attempt <= maxIndexAttempts; attempt++) {
  1546	    let promotedToFinalPath = false;
  1547	    let handledFailureWhileLocked = false;
  1548	    let rollbackSucceededAfterRejectedSave = false;
  1549	    try {
  1550	      const prepared = prepareParsedMemoryForIndexing(
  1551	        memoryParser.parseMemoryContent(file_path, content),
  1552	        database,
  1553	      );
  1554	
  1555	      if (!prepared.validation.valid) {
  1556	        validationError = prepared.validation;
  1557	        indexError = null;
  1558	        break;
  1559	      }
  1560	
  1561	      if (prepared.qualityLoopResult.fixes.length > 0 && prepared.qualityLoopResult.passed && prepared.qualityLoopResult.fixedContent) {
  1562	        console.error(`[memory-save] Quality loop applied ${prepared.qualityLoopResult.fixes.length} auto-fix(es) for ${path.basename(file_path)} before pending-file promotion`);
  1563	      }
  1564	
  1565	      const persistedContent = prepared.qualityLoopResult.passed && prepared.qualityLoopResult.fixedContent
  1566	        ? prepared.qualityLoopResult.fixedContent
  1567	        : content;
  1568	
  1569	      indexResult = await withSpecFolderLock(prepared.parsed.specFolder, async () => {
  1570	        try {
  1571	          fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(pendingPath), { recursive: true });
  1572	          fs.writeFileSync(pendingPath, persistedContent, 'utf-8');
  1573	          fs.renameSync(pendingPath, file_path);
  1574	          promotedToFinalPath = true;
  1575	
  1576	          const lockedIndexResult = await processPreparedMemory(prepared, file_path, {
  1577	            force,
  1578	            asyncEmbedding: true,
  1579	            persistQualityLoopContent: false,
  1580	            specFolderLockAlreadyHeld: true,
  1581	          });
  1582	
  1583	          if (lockedIndexResult.status === 'rejected') {
  1584	            handledFailureWhileLocked = true;
  1585	            const rollbackResult = restoreAtomicSaveOriginalState(file_path, originalState);
  1586	            rollbackSucceededAfterRejectedSave = rollbackResult.restored;
  1587	            if (!rollbackResult.restored && rollbackResult.error) {
  1588	              mergeErrorMetadata({ rollbackError: rollbackResult.error });
  1589	            }
  1590	          }
  1591	
  1592	          return lockedIndexResult;
  1593	        } catch (lockedError: unknown) {
  1594	          handledFailureWhileLocked = true;
  1595	          const pendingCleanupResult = cleanupAtomicSavePendingFile(pendingPath);
  1596	          if (!pendingCleanupResult.cleaned && pendingCleanupResult.error) {
  1597	            mergeErrorMetadata({ pendingCleanupError: pendingCleanupResult.error });
  1598	          }
  1599	          if (promotedToFinalPath) {
  1600	            const rollbackResult = restoreAtomicSaveOriginalState(file_path, originalState);
  1601	            restoredOriginalAfterFailure = rollbackResult.restored;
  1602	            if (!rollbackResult.restored && rollbackResult.error) {
  1603	              mergeErrorMetadata({ rollbackError: rollbackResult.error });
  1604	            }
  1605	            if (!restoredOriginalAfterFailure) {
  1606	              const promoteMessage = lockedError instanceof Error ? lockedError.message : String(lockedError);
  1607	              throw new Error(`Original file rollback failed after promote-before-index path: ${promoteMessage}`);
  1608	            }
  1609	          }
  1610	          throw lockedError;
  1611	        }
  1612	      });
  1613	      if (indexResult.status === 'error') {
  1614	        throw new Error(indexResult.message ?? indexResult.error ?? 'indexMemoryFile returned status=error');
  1615	      }

     1	# Sync — Realign This Scaffold
     2	
     3	## Recommended: Use sync.sh
     4	
     5	```bash
     6	.mex/sync.sh
     7	```
     8	
     9	The script runs drift detection first, shows you exactly what's wrong, then offers:
    10	1. **Targeted sync** — AI fixes only the flagged files (fastest, cheapest)
    11	2. **Full resync** — AI re-reads everything and updates all scaffold files
    12	3. **Prompt export** — shows the prompts for manual paste
    13	4. **Exit** — fix it yourself
    14	
    15	## Quick Check
    16	
    17	```bash
    18	mex check              # full drift report
    19	mex check --quiet      # one-liner: "drift score 85/100 (1 error)"
    20	mex sync --dry-run     # preview targeted fix prompts
    21	```
    22	
    23	## Manual Resync
    24	
    25	If you prefer to paste a prompt manually, or don't have the CLI built:
    26	
    27	---
    28	
    29	**SYNC PROMPT — copy everything between the lines:**
    30	
    31	```
    32	You are going to resync the AI context scaffold for this project.
    33	The scaffold may be out of date — the codebase has changed since it was last populated.
    34	
    35	First, read all files in context/ to understand the current scaffold state.
    36	Then explore what has changed in the codebase since the scaffold was last updated.
    37	Check the last_updated dates in the YAML frontmatter of each file.
    38	
    39	For each context/ file:
    40	1. Compare the current scaffold content to the actual codebase
    41	2. Identify what has changed, been added, or been removed
    42	3. Update the file to reflect the current state
    43	
    44	Critical rules for updating:
    45	- Use surgical, targeted edits — NOT full file rewrites. Read the existing content,
    46	  identify what changed, and update only those sections.
    47	- PRESERVE YAML frontmatter structure. Never delete or rewrite the entire frontmatter block.
    48	  Edit individual fields only. The edges, triggers, name, and description fields must
    49	  survive every sync. If you need to update edges, add or remove individual entries —
    50	  do not replace the entire array.
    51	- In context/decisions.md: NEVER delete existing decisions.
    52	  If a decision has changed, mark the old entry as "Superseded by [new decision title]"
    53	  and add the new decision above it with today's date.
    54	- In all other files: update content to reflect current reality
    55	- Update last_updated in the YAML frontmatter of every file you change
    56	- After updating each file, update ROUTER.md Current Project State
    57	
    58	When done, report:
    59	- Which files were updated and what changed
    60	- Any decisions that were superseded
    61	- Any slots that could not be filled with confidence
    62	```

codex
The core contrast is holding up: Mex is strongly isolated by project root and scaffold directory, but it doesn’t appear to implement actor-aware scoping or conflict resolution beyond “prompt a human/AI and re-check.” I’m pulling one more pass on checkpoint/conflict handling in Spec Kit so the merge-conflict part is grounded too.

     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Spec Folder Mutex
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// Per-spec-folder save mutex to prevent concurrent indexing races (TOCTOU).
     5	
     6	// Feature catalog: Memory indexing (memory_save)
     7	// Feature catalog: Atomic-save parity and partial-indexing hints
     8	
     9	/** Per-spec-folder save mutex to prevent concurrent indexing races (TOCTOU) */
    10	const SPEC_FOLDER_LOCKS = new Map<string, Promise<unknown>>();
    11	
    12	async function withSpecFolderLock<T>(specFolder: string, fn: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T> {
    13	  const normalizedFolder = specFolder || '__global__';
    14	  const chain = (SPEC_FOLDER_LOCKS.get(normalizedFolder) ?? Promise.resolve())
    15	    .catch((error: unknown) => {
    16	      console.error('[memory-save] prior queued save failed:', error);
    17	    })
    18	    .then(() => fn());
    19	  SPEC_FOLDER_LOCKS.set(normalizedFolder, chain);
    20	  try {
    21	    return await chain;
    22	  } finally {
    23	    if (SPEC_FOLDER_LOCKS.get(normalizedFolder) === chain) {
    24	      SPEC_FOLDER_LOCKS.delete(normalizedFolder);
    25	    }
    26	  }
    27	}
    28	
    29	export { SPEC_FOLDER_LOCKS, withSpecFolderLock };

     1	import chalk from "chalk";
     2	import { Command } from "commander";
     3	import { findConfig } from "./config.js";
     4	import { reportConsole, reportQuiet, reportJSON } from "./reporter.js";
     5	
     6	const program = new Command();
     7	
     8	program
     9	  .name("mex")
    10	  .description("CLI engine for mex scaffold — drift detection, pre-analysis, and targeted sync")
    11	  .version("0.4.0");
    12	
    13	// ── Setup (npx entry point) ──
    14	program
    15	  .command("setup")
    16	  .description("First-time setup — create .mex/ scaffold and populate with AI")
    17	  .option("--dry-run", "Show what would happen without making changes")
    18	  .action(async (opts) => {
    19	    try {
    20	      const { runSetup } = await import("./setup/index.js");
    21	      await runSetup({ dryRun: opts.dryRun });
    22	    } catch (err) {
    23	      console.error((err as Error).message);
    24	      process.exit(1);
    25	    }
    26	  });
    27	
    28	// ── Layer 2: Drift Detection ──
    29	program
    30	  .command("check")
    31	  .description("Detect drift between scaffold files and codebase reality")
    32	  .option("--json", "Output full drift report as JSON")
    33	  .option("--quiet", "Single-line summary only")
    34	  .option("--fix", "Run sync to fix any issues found")
    35	  .action(async (opts) => {
    36	    try {
    37	      const config = findConfig();
    38	      const { runDriftCheck } = await import("./drift/index.js");
    39	      const report = await runDriftCheck(config);
    40	
    41	      if (opts.json) {
    42	        reportJSON(report);
    43	      } else if (opts.quiet) {
    44	        reportQuiet(report);
    45	      } else {
    46	        reportConsole(report);
    47	      }
    48	
    49	      // If --fix and there are issues, jump to sync
    50	      const hasErrors = report.issues.some((i) => i.severity === "error");
    51	      if (opts.fix && hasErrors) {
    52	        const { runSync } = await import("./sync/index.js");
    53	        await runSync(config, {});
    54	        return;
    55	      }
    56	
    57	      if (hasErrors) process.exit(1);
    58	    } catch (err) {
    59	      console.error((err as Error).message);
    60	      process.exit(1);
    61	    }
    62	  });
    63	
    64	// ── Layer 1: Pre-analysis Scanner ──
    65	program
    66	  .command("init")
    67	  .description("Scan codebase and generate pre-analysis brief for AI")
    68	  .option("--json", "Output scanner brief as JSON")
    69	  .action(async (opts) => {
    70	    try {
    71	      const config = findConfig();
    72	      const { runScan } = await import("./scanner/index.js");
    73	      const result = await runScan(config, { jsonOnly: opts.json });
    74	
    75	      if (opts.json) {
    76	        console.log(JSON.stringify(result, null, 2));
    77	      } else {
    78	        console.log(result);
    79	      }

    81	      console.error((err as Error).message);
    82	      process.exit(1);
    83	    }
    84	  });
    85	
    86	// ── Layer 3: Targeted Sync ──
    87	program
    88	  .command("sync")

    90	  .option("--dry-run", "Show what would be synced without executing")

    92	  .action(async (opts) => {

# Iteration 025: MULTI-AGENT MEMORY SAFETY

## Focus
MULTI-AGENT MEMORY SAFETY: How does this system handle concurrent agent access, merge conflicts, project isolation, and shared vs private memory scoping?

## Findings
### Finding 1: Mex isolates by project root, not by actor, tenant, or memory visibility
- **Source**: [README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L34), [README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L94), [types.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/types.ts#L5), [setup/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/setup/index.ts#L58)
- **What it does**: Mex anchors everything to one `projectRoot` and one `.mex/` `scaffoldRoot`. The CLI is explicitly run from project root, and setup discovers the nearest `.git` root before creating the scaffold.
- **Why it matters**: This gives clean project isolation, but it is only filesystem isolation. I found no tenant, user, agent, shared-space, private-scope, or access-control model in the Mex runtime surface.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 2: Mex has no real concurrent-writer safety for scaffold mutations
- **Source**: [pattern/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/pattern/index.ts#L11), [watch.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/watch.ts#L41), [sync/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L29)
- **What it does**: `pattern add` writes the new file and appends to `patterns/INDEX.md` with direct sync file ops. `watch` appends or rewrites `.git/hooks/post-commit`. `sync` assumes one interactive Claude repair session, then re-checks drift.
- **Why it matters**: Mex behaves like a single-writer markdown scaffold. Two agents updating patterns, `INDEX.md`, or hook content at the same time could race, and there is no mutex, version check, or conflict ledger to catch that.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 3: Mex’s “merge/conflict” model is really repair prompting, not storage-level conflict resolution
- **Source**: [SYNC.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/SYNC.md#L9), [sync/brief-builder.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts#L18), [git.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/git.ts#L52)
- **What it does**: When drift is found, Mex builds a repair brief from the current file, issue list, nearby filesystem context, and recent git diffs, then asks AI to fix only the flagged files.
- **Why it matters**: This is useful after a conflict or drift event, but it does not prevent concurrent writes or resolve competing memory versions. It is a recovery workflow, not a merge-control system.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 4: Spec Kit Memory already has explicit shared vs private scoping with deny-by-default access control
- **Source**: [README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md#L893), [README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md#L1703), [shared-spaces.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/collab/shared-spaces.ts#L507), [shared-memory.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/shared-memory.ts#L144), [stage1-candidate-gen.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/stage1-candidate-gen.ts#L985)
- **What it does**: Shared memory is opt-in. Spaces start locked, membership is role-based, caller identity must be exactly one user or agent, and retrieval filters candidates by `tenantId`, `userId`, `agentId`, and allowed `sharedSpaceId`s before ranking continues.
- **Why it matters**: This is the concrete multi-agent safety layer Mex lacks. Spec Kit distinguishes private scope from collaborative scope at query time and mutation time, rather than relying on directory boundaries alone.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 5: Spec Kit Memory also has real concurrent-write and conflict controls that Mex does not
- **Source**: [context-server.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L1632), [spec-folder-mutex.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/spec-folder-mutex.ts#L1), [memory-save.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1513), [shared-spaces.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/collab/shared-spaces.ts#L135), [shared-spaces.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/collab/shared-spaces.ts#L678), [vector-index-schema.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-schema.ts#L1503)
- **What it does**: The server forces SQLite WAL mode, `memory_save` serializes writes with a per-spec-folder mutex, uses unique pending-file suffixes to avoid pending-file races, logs shared-space conflicts into `shared_space_conflicts`, and escalates repeat or high-risk collisions from `append_version` to `manual_merge`.
- **Why it matters**: This is a genuine concurrency and merge-conflict story: serialize same-scope saves, preserve data, and retain an auditable record when shared writes collide. That is materially stronger than Mex’s “repair after drift” approach.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 6: Checkpoint restore adds another safety boundary by blocking saves and rolling back failed merge restores
- **Source**: [memory-save.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1080), [checkpoints.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/checkpoints.ts#L1097), [checkpoints.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/checkpoints.ts#L1855)
- **What it does**: `memory_save` refuses writes while checkpoint restore maintenance is active, and merge-mode restore uses savepoints so reinsertion failures roll back instead of leaving partially restored scoped data behind.
- **Why it matters**: This is important multi-agent safety during maintenance windows. It prevents a restore from interleaving with live writes and turning scoped memories or shared-space state into mixed snapshots.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high

## Assessment
- New information ratio: 0.48

## Recommended Next Focus
Compare these backend safeguards against the user-facing surface. The next high-value pass is: how should `memory_search`, `memory_context`, and `/spec_kit:resume` expose shared-space conflicts, degraded trust, or `manual_merge` requirements so multi-agent collisions become visible workflow state instead of remaining mostly internal audit data.

### Finding 2: Mex has no real concurrent-writer safety for scaffold mutations
- **Source**: [pattern/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/pattern/index.ts#L11), [watch.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/watch.ts#L41), [sync/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L29)
- **What it does**: `pattern add` writes the new file and appends to `patterns/INDEX.md` with direct sync file ops. `watch` appends or rewrites `.git/hooks/post-commit`. `sync` assumes one interactive Claude repair session, then re-checks drift.
- **Why it matters**: Mex behaves like a single-writer markdown scaffold. Two agents updating patterns, `INDEX.md`, or hook content at the same time could race, and there is no mutex, version check, or conflict ledger to catch that.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 3: Mex’s “merge/conflict” model is really repair prompting, not storage-level conflict resolution
- **Source**: [SYNC.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/SYNC.md#L9), [sync/brief-builder.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts#L18), [git.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/git.ts#L52)
- **What it does**: When drift is found, Mex builds a repair brief from the current file, issue list, nearby filesystem context, and recent git diffs, then asks AI to fix only the flagged files.
- **Why it matters**: This is useful after a conflict or drift event, but it does not prevent concurrent writes or resolve competing memory versions. It is a recovery workflow, not a merge-control system.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 4: Spec Kit Memory already has explicit shared vs private scoping with deny-by-default access control
- **Source**: [README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md#L893), [README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md#L1703), [shared-spaces.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/collab/shared-spaces.ts#L507), [shared-memory.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/shared-memory.ts#L144), [stage1-candidate-gen.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/stage1-candidate-gen.ts#L985)
- **What it does**: Shared memory is opt-in. Spaces start locked, membership is role-based, caller identity must be exactly one user or agent, and retrieval filters candidates by `tenantId`, `userId`, `agentId`, and allowed `sharedSpaceId`s before ranking continues.
- **Why it matters**: This is the concrete multi-agent safety layer Mex lacks. Spec Kit distinguishes private scope from collaborative scope at query time and mutation time, rather than relying on directory boundaries alone.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 5: Spec Kit Memory also has real concurrent-write and conflict controls that Mex does not
- **Source**: [context-server.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L1632), [spec-folder-mutex.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/spec-folder-mutex.ts#L1), [memory-save.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1513), [shared-spaces.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/collab/shared-spaces.ts#L135), [shared-spaces.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/collab/shared-spaces.ts#L678), [vector-index-schema.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-schema.ts#L1503)
- **What it does**: The server forces SQLite WAL mode, `memory_save` serializes writes with a per-spec-folder mutex, uses unique pending-file suffixes to avoid pending-file races, logs shared-space conflicts into `shared_space_conflicts`, and escalates repeat or high-risk collisions from `append_version` to `manual_merge`.
- **Why it matters**: This is a genuine concurrency and merge-conflict story: serialize same-scope saves, preserve data, and retain an auditable record when shared writes collide. That is materially stronger than Mex’s “repair after drift” approach.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 6: Checkpoint restore adds another safety boundary by blocking saves and rolling back failed merge restores
- **Source**: [memory-save.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1080), [checkpoints.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/checkpoints.ts#L1097), [checkpoints.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/checkpoints.ts#L1855)
- **What it does**: `memory_save` refuses writes while checkpoint restore maintenance is active, and merge-mode restore uses savepoints so reinsertion failures roll back instead of leaving partially restored scoped data behind.
- **Why it matters**: This is important multi-agent safety during maintenance windows. It prevents a restore from interleaving with live writes and turning scoped memories or shared-space state into mixed snapshots.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high

## Assessment
- New information ratio: 0.48

## Recommended Next Focus
Compare these backend safeguards against the user-facing surface. The next high-value pass is: how should `memory_search`, `memory_context`, and `/spec_kit:resume` expose shared-space conflicts, degraded trust, or `manual_merge` requirements so multi-agent collisions become visible workflow state instead of remaining mostly internal audit data.
